abstract.text: A device-only face recognition SDK for iOS.
Objective-C-visible metadata accessors for the YEOFR framework.
Fuses CNN + multi-signal anti-spoof + depth into a single
hardware get the same shape as those with it.
Public handle type alias for the underlying  tracker (HTracker).
the face actually crossing the edge to signal “too close”, but
on an iPhone selfie camera the face usually fills most of the
frame before any edge gets touched, so
and users get no feedback as they move the phone too near. A
width-fraction ceiling gives the UX prompt a chance to appear
before the rect escapes the frame entirely.
Creates a new tracker and installs it as the current handle.

Per-frame corroborating evidence the caller can supply so the SDK
Snapshot of every signal-fed value plus the accumulator’s
derived trust verdict for one frame. Broadcast via
required for the depth-true override. Photo/screen attacks that
somehow fool depth rarely pass temporal+flicker floors together,
so 0.5 is conservative. Missing signals (pre-warmup) are treated
as 0 for the override (see the header comment) — the override
refuses to fire until the ring buffer has actually warmed up.
Public per-frame fusion trace for debug UI / CSV capture.

differs from the raw fused decision.

consumers can render distinct UX for “no face”, “too close”, etc.,
instead of treating every non-evaluable frame as a spoof.
is the synchronous FR pass for this frame; the evaluation half is
trust verdict (which may incorporate prior frames’
liveness results — the CNN runs asynchronously, so this frame’s
CNN verdict typically lands ~1 frame later).

Primitives the trust decision needs. Kept free of SDK verdict
types so the boundary is easy to drive from tests and so

Frees the current tracker (if any) and clears the handle.
Detects faces in a single ARGB32 image frame passed as a Base64-encoded buffer.
